I don’t know what they are planning, but it should be a fun time, knowing the Trinity folk. Sabbaticals, if you’re unaware of them, are based on the Sabbath concept of the 7th day, except years are used. In academia, churches, and some businesses, leaders are given a portion of every 7th year to take time off for study, rest, and reflection. summary: Meet Nala, a 3-year old shepherd-mix. She's shy at first but just a wait a few minutes and you’ll find that she's smart, curious and attentive.

She also asked the plaintiffs and the state discuss whether a full or partial settlement of the case was possible.

title: Falling oil prices, revenue forecast paint gloomy picture for Alaska

title: While not highly prized, coyote fur still works great for Alaskans
summary: The coyote population has maintained relatively normal fluctuations based on cycles of their prey, primarily snowshoe hares and voles. When these animals are scarce, coyotes will eat a variety of things. Carrion from wolf kills ranks high.

title: Gomez Provides a Lift in Devils Encore
summary: Scott Gomez had been skating with the New Jersey Devils on a professional tryout since August, and his patience finally paid off this month when he signed a one-year deal with the club, which has been plagued by inconsistent goal scoring, a rash of injuries and a flu bug that circulated through its dressing room.

title: Alaska's comeback falls short in 5-4 overtime loss to Idaho
summary: Alaska rallied from a 4-1 deficit but allowed Idaho a power-play goal in overtime to drop a 5-4 ECHL hockey game on Saturday night at Sullivan Arena.

title: Chris Thompson: 2 events highlight work of Anchorage's Methodists
summary: A series of talks by a prominent theologian and a conference on how churches can address special needs highlight the work being done by Methodists in Southcentral Alaska.

title: Lack of competition keeps Alaska gas costly
summary: Every Alaskan knows the feeling. You see a commercial on TV advertising a great new deal, but in small print at the bottom of the screen, "except in Alaska or Hawaii." Many Alaskans accept this as a cost of living in our great state. But there is one area where this shouldn't apply and that's gasoline prices.

title: Justice Department's new racial profiling policy should go further, some say

title: Despite low water and wind-blown grit, dipnetters seek silver treasure at Chitina

There is no truth to the rumor. No truth at all. The Alaska Department of Fish and Game's preseason forecast for the Kenai calls for a return of 6.1 million sockeye salmon this year, and early indications from the Copper are that sockeye, or red salmon, runs might be coming back a little stronger than projected. Given the sheer size of the return, state fisheries biologists said, it should be obvious to everyone there is no threat to July's hugely popular Kenai dipnet fishery. Common sense alone dictates t…

title: House bipartisan budget deal appears to be dead on arrival in the Senate
summary: A budget deal painstakingly negotiated in the Alaska House dropped dead on arrival in the Senate on Saturday, raising doubts about the Legislature’s ability to finish its business before layoff notices are mailed to 10,000 state workers Monday.

title: Body of missing Fairbanks teen found after intensive search
summary: A wide-ranging community search for a missing 16-year-old Fairbanks girl ended Friday when the girl’s body was found near her family’s home. She had committed suicide, her mother said.

title: Alaska's US senators should know better than to vote away global warming
summary: OPINION: Someone needs to tell Alaska's U.S. senators that the conclusions of science -- and the problems they seek to understand -- can't be voted on.

title: 54 climbers summited Denali on Father's Day
summary: Light wind and clouds that cleared briefly in the afternoon greeted Mount McKinley climbers on June 20. Fifty-four climbers seized the opportunity to reach the summit that Father's Day.

title: 'Duty to produce' Alaska's natural gas? Just shoot me already.
summary: OPINION: It's damn sad we are this deep into the weeds debating if the state can force a gas pipeline into existence. Which it can't, just to be clear.

title: Moose kicks woman who came too close in Town Square
summary: A moose kicked a woman in Town Square Park Monday afternoon after she got too close to the animal and reportedly tried to pet it, according to Anchorage police.

title: In playoff contention, Aces literally have a long road ahead
summary: After a miserable start to the ECHL season and plenty of rough patches along the way, the Alaska Aces have gone 14-5-3 in their last 22 games and given themselves a legitimate shot at the playoffs heading into a grinding 10-game road trip.

title: 1A girls: Wainwright, Buckland advance to final
summary: Wainwright dominated Teller 64-29 Tuesday in a Class 1A girls semifinal game to earn a spot in Wednesday's championship game at the Alaska Airlines Center. The Huskies will meet Buckland at 2 p.m. Wednesday.

title: Alaskans qualify for world juniors
summary: The snowstorm that pummeled the East Coast this week tried its best to bury Anchorage biathlete Sam Dougherty's hopes of winning a spot in the World Junior Championships. It delayed and even disarmed him, but Dougherty and Alaska Biathlon teammate Ray Wonders nonetheless took the U.S. team trials by storm this week in Jericho, Vt.

title: Alaska Sports Hall of Fame honors trailblazers, trail runner, trail maker
summary: Dick Mize, Nancy Pease and John Brown headline the Alaska Sports Hall of Fame's Class of 2015, which also includes Michaela Hutchison's 2006 state wrestling championship and the Iron Dog snowmachine race.

title: As clock ticks toward shutdown, Alaska Legislature can't resolve budget
summary: With a government shutdown looming July 1 and layoff notices to 10,000 state employees set to be mailed Monday, the Alaska Legislature remained locked in inaction Sunday.

title: Miller vows silence on questions about personal background
summary: Republican Senate nominee Joe Miller said Monday he will not be answering any more questions about his personal background for the rest of the campaign.

title: Alaska Supreme Court backs group fighting Pebble mine
summary: In two unanimous decisions, the Alaska Supreme Court on Friday came down solidly on the side of a group fighting the proposed Pebble mine, backing efforts by two Alaska icons to give the public a voice in mineral exploration.
